title: Roni von Wasaki
content:
{{Top|ffa|ncl}}
{{Youmay|the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity|the [[canon]] [[farmer]] [[Roni]]|linebreak=1}}
{{Unlicensed}}
{{Character
|type=Jedi
|image=[[File:RoniVonWasaki-Challenge66.png]]

|homeworld=

|death=

{{Quote|You ''must help me''—I am the light side of the Force! If I am killed, all that is good and true will be extinguished from the galaxy forever!|Roni von Wasaki, to one of the individuals who unfroze him from carbonite|<ref name="Date" />}}
A [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] active prior to [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]], '''Roni von Wasaki''' was respected as both an able [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] and a skilled warrior. In response to a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] placed on Wasaki by a [[Unidentified crime boss|crime boss]], the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Leshy Drobo]] and his [[Assassin droid/Legends|droid]] partner, [[XT-8]], were ultimately successful in capturing the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], whom they [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|froze]] in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] for [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] to the bounty's originator. Wasaki retained his mental faculties and the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|ability]] to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], however, and [[Mind trick/Legends|influenced]] Drobo's [[Brain/Legends|mind]] so that he would release him.

Wasaki's plan backfired, however, when XT-8 confronted and [[Death/Legends|killed]] his partner, in the process damaging the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] of the ''[[Starkiller]]'', the [[Frommon longhaul all-purpose transport|starship]] they were traveling aboard. XT-8 then simply continued the journey at subliminal speed, leading the journey to stretch on for twenty [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]], an ordeal during which the [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] Wasaki went insane. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], another [[Starship/Legends|starship]] discovered the ''Starkiller'' and its crew eventually released Wasaki from carbonite. His madness and growing paranoia soon caused Wasaki to attack his liberators, but they ultimately managed to calm his mind by using the Force.

==Biography==
===Captured===
{{Quote|Ambush went well. ''[…]'' While Wasaki concentrated on me, XT-8 snuck behind and stunned him. ''[…]'' Wasaki now stored in hold, waiting delivery and my 100,000 creds.|Leshy Drobo's personal log entry|<ref name="Date" />}}
Roni von Wasaki was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] active<ref name="Disturbance" /> at some point prior to [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]].<ref name="Date" /> He established order in many of the [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] that formed the frontier of the [[galactic community]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]]. Eventually, a [[Unidentified crime boss|crime boss]] posted a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 100,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] head. Subsequently, many [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] failed to capture Wasaki, until [[Leshy Drobo]] and his partners, [[Gammid]] and the [[XT labor droid]]-turned-[[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]], [[XT-8]], laid an ambush for the Jedi. Although Gammid [[Death/Legends|died]] during the confrontation, XT-8 managed to sneak behind Wasaki and stun him while the latter was concentrating on Drobo.<ref name="Disturbance" />

[[File:XT8-Challenge66.png|thumb|right|300px|The droid XT-8 managed to stun Roni von Wasaki, leading to the latter's capture and imprisonment.]]
Drobo then confiscated Wasaki's pair of [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], which he stored in the [[Captain/Legends|captain's]] quarters of his [[Frommon longhaul all-purpose transport|freighter]], the ''[[Starkiller]]'', and then he and XT-8 [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|froze]] the Jedi into a slab of [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. Wasaki's [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] form was then stashed in a secret compartment of the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} cargo hold for [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] to the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who had originated the bounty.<ref name="Disturbance" />

Frozen in carbonite, Wasaki remained alive and in perfect hibernation and, by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], managed to keep his mind focused. While Drobo was noting Wasaki's capture in an entry of his personal log, the Jedi begun to subtly [[Mind trick/Legends|influence]] the bounty [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] via [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]] to release him from the carbonite, although Drobo quickly realized the danger posed by that idea. Shortly after the ''Starkiller'' had jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], however, Wasaki finally implanted a similar suggestion in Drobo's mind, with the bounty hunter beginning to act upon it. However, XT-8 learned of its partner's intentions and confronted Drobo in the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter's]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]] room, which led to the former killing the latter, in the process damaging the [[Starship/Legends|starship's]] [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. As a result, the ship reverted to [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]].<ref name="Disturbance" />

===A journey of millennia===
Since repairing the ''Starkiller'' was beyond XT-8's [[Programmer/Legends|programming]], the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] simply continued its mission under sublight speed, turning a brief [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] trip into a more than twenty-[[Millennium/Legends|thousand-year]]-long journey. While Wasaki remained hidden in the ''Starkiller''{{'s}} cargo hold,<ref name="Disturbance" /> the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[6900 BBY]] saw the [[Exiles|beginnings]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], which was an order of practitioners of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] that eventually became the ideological opposite of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the [[Order of the Sith Lords|Sith]] [[Grand Plan/Legends|brought about]] the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|virtual extermination]] of the Jedi Order and the [[Declaration of a New Order|replacement]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], which the Jedi had served for twenty-five thousand years, with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

During the intervening millennia, Wasaki continued to reach out via the Force in order to contact anyone who could liberate him. The perpetual loneliness, fearful [[Dream/Legends|dreams]], and extreme sensory deprivation that the Jedi endured, however, eventually drove him mad.<ref name="Disturbance" />

{{RPG|"[[Disturbance in the Force (article)|Disturbance in the Force]]"}}
===Liberation from imprisonment===
{{Quote|Not alone…found living souls. Can't escape…can't…leave me alone…|Roni von Wasaki, making telepathic contact with one of the crew-members of the starship that encountered the ''Starkiller''|<ref name="Date" />}}
[[File:StarkillerDeckplan-Challenge66.png|thumb|left|300px|The deckplan of the ''Starkiller'', aboard which Roni von Wasaki spent twenty thousand years.]]
At some point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="Disturbance" /> fought between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> another starship traveling through hyperspace unintentionally emerged in realspace close to the ''Starkiller''. Attempts by a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] member of the other vessel's crew to discern the presence of any living beings aboard the ''Starkiller'' were unsuccessful, although at that point Wasaki established telepathic contact with one of the crew-members. In the brief communication, the Jedi only managed to vaguely and almost incoherently point to his situation.<ref name="Disturbance" />

The group then boarded the ''Starkiller'' and, during their exploration of the ship, discovered the secret compartment in the cargo hold where the frozen body of Wasaki was located. After de-carbonizing the twenty-thousand-year-old Jedi, the explorers made him comfortable while at the same time attempting to question him, and subsequently both they and the ancient Jedi left the ''Starkiller'' and departed aboard the starship the group had arrived on.<ref name="Disturbance" />

===A mad Jedi===
{{Quote|It is here! It has found me and is ''on this ship''!|Roni von Wasaki, to one of the individuals who unfroze him from carbonite, on a supposed physical manifestation of the dark side of the Force|<ref name="Date" />}}
Shortly after the ship entered hyperspace, Wasaki became fearful and, taking one of the vessel's crew-members aside, claimed that something that was also aboard the same ship had "found" him. In response to intense questioning by the individual, the Jedi further asked for help in fighting a physical manifestation of the dark side of the Force that had supposedly followed Wasaki aboard the starship. Noticing that the crew-member was unresponsive to his pleas, Wasaki then stated that he was an embodiment of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] and claimed that his death would lead to the disappearance from [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] of "all that [was] good and true."<ref name="Disturbance" />

When the crew of the ship realized that Wasaki's [[Brain/Legends|mind]] was disturbed and began planning on how to deal with him, the Jedi entered a catatonic state. Standing rigid with his [[Limb/Legends|hands]] held out before him—in a posture that resembled someone who had been frozen in carbonite—Wasaki resisted all attempts to move him and began using various [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]] to attack the crew-members. The Jedi then suddenly snapped out of that bizarre state and continued with the offensive, complementing the Force powers with direct attacks with a lightsaber. Eventually, the crew-members managed to calm the mad Jedi's mind via the Force, and one of the individuals requested that Wasaki provide instruction in the use of the Force.<ref name="Disturbance" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
===Imprisoned Jedi===
{{Quote|Wasaki's as tricky as a Lwen'len Wingor.|Leshy Drobo's personal log entry|<ref name="Date" />}}
A short [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] with light skin, dark [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and a waxy face, Roni von Wasaki was originally respected by peaceful individuals for his just [[Administrator/Legends|administration]]. He was knowledgeable in [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucracy]] and adept at balancing wisdom and peace with a struggle for justice, and even after his capture by the bounty hunter Leshy Drobo, the latter considered the former as "tricky" as a [[Lwen'len Wingor]].<ref name="Disturbance" />

The twenty thousand years Wasaki spent frozen in carbonite, which induced in him long, perpetual loneliness, fearful dreams, and extreme sensory deprivation, eventually drove the Jedi mad. During his imprisonment, the captive had been reaching out telepathically in search of help for so long that, eventually, he could no longer control the use of that ability, leading to him constantly attempting telepathic contact with random individuals. When Wasaki finally managed to contact one of his future liberators, the individual was overcome with a chaotic flood of the Jedi's thoughts and fears, amid which Wasaki repeatedly expressed his loneliness and desperation.<ref name="Disturbance" />

===Liberated madman===
By the time he was released from carbonite, Wasaki had also been rendered permanently blind due to a severe case of [[Hibernation sickness/Legends|hibernation sickness]]. Upon his release, Wasaki was devoid of energy and did not express any emotions. He did not volunteer any information to his liberators, and, when asked a question, he repeated it once or twice before answering in the form of brief, confused fits of rambling.<ref name="Disturbance" />

Soon, Wasaki's paranoia partially contributed to the Jedi experiencing the feeling that he was watched and talked about by someone, and that someone was attempting to enter his mind without permission. He began claiming that a physical manifestation of the dark side of the Force was following him with the intention of killing him. Wasaki also claimed that he embodied the light side of the Force and was not above using the Force to make his listener more receptive to his claims. Eventually, the Jedi's madness caused him to attack the group of individuals that had rescued him from the carbonite imprisonment, although he had not yet succumbed to the dark side of the Force at that point. After the group succeeded in calming Wasaki's mind, the Jedi's former personality began to re-emerge.<ref name="Disturbance" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|Maybe I should let him out—he's harmless without blades. No! What am I thinking.|Leshy Drobo, on Roni von Wasaki in his personal log entry|<ref name="Date" />}}
[[File:SamuraiDelicatessenInSpace-Challenge66.png|thumb|right|300px|Roni von Wasaki, armed and armored]]
Roni von Wasaki was a warrior skilled enough to be hated and feared by the villains of his time. He was adept at climbing and jumping and was able to simultaneously [[Jar'Kai/Legends|use]] two lightsabers in combat. Wasaki was also a skilled user of various Force powers, such as [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]] and the more offensive [[Inflict Pain]] and [[Injure/Kill]]. The Jedi could also use the Force to telepathically communicate with other people and affect their minds.<ref name="Disturbance" />

Upon being frozen in carbonite, Wasaki drew on the Force to keep his mind focused. He was able to implant subtle suggestions in Leshy Drobo's mind, but the reputation of his skills backfired when Drobo realized the incongruity of the thought that Wasaki was "harmless" without access to his lightsabers. Over the subsequent twenty millennia, the imprisoned Jedi constantly attempted telepathic contact with anyone who could potentially free him, being able to make his communication attempts overtake an individual's own thoughts and senses and render the receiver's body rigid.<ref name="Disturbance" />

In order to compensate for the blindness induced in him by hibernation sickness, Wasaki learned to [[Force sense/Legends|see]] by using the Force. The Jedi's Force skills were not able to protect his mind from madness caused by the imprisonment in carbonite, however. Ultimately, at one point following his release, Wasaki briefly fell into a catatonic state during which he became unaffected by pain.<ref name="Disturbance" />

==Equipment==
Roni von Wasaki's attire was stylistically similar to that of other ancient Jedi. He wore [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Jedi apparel/Legends|robes]] with a [[Belt/Legends|belt]], under which was a light-colored tunic; black antique [[Jedi armor|armor]] on his chest, legs, and shoulders; and an ornate horned [[Helmet/Legends|helm]]. Wasaki wielded two lightsabers, of which one had a three-[[Foot/Legends|feet]]-long blade and the other, a less-powerful left-hand weapon, only a [[Shoto lightsaber/Legends|two-feet-long]] one. The pommel of Wasaki's main lightsaber's [[Lightsaber hilt/Legends|hilt]] was decorated with a [[Ring/Legends|ring]] to which several ribbons were attached.<ref name="Disturbance" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Disturbance_in_the_Force.png|thumb|right|300px|Roni von Wasaki was introduced in the roleplaying mini-adventure "Disturbance in the Force."]]
Roni von Wasaki was introduced in "[[Disturbance in the Force (article)|Disturbance in the Force]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] mini-adventure written by [[Chris Hind]] as a supplement for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', illustrated by [[Paul Daly]], and published in [[Challenge 66|''Challenge'' 66]] in [[November]] [[1992]]. In the adventure, if the player characters are unsuccessful at calming Wasaki's mind when he has begun attacking them, his madness leads him to completely turn to the dark side of the Force, and it is suggested that, at a later point, they can encounter the now-evil Jedi as an agent of the Galactic Empire. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="Disturbance" />

"Disturbance in the Force," which was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process and never had its [[canon]]icity within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity confirmed, described Wasaki as wielding lightsabers<ref name="Disturbance" /> prior to 20,000 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> a fact which was apparently contradicted when the [[2005]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' established that the [[Protosaber|earliest such weapons]] were only invented around [[15,500 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" /> Furthermore, the ''Challenge'' 66 mini-adventure depicts Wasaki's lightsabers without any visible external [[Power cell/Legends|power packs]],<ref name="Disturbance" /> a technological development of Jedi lightsaber technology the introduction of which ''The New Essential Chronology'' places around [[4800 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" />
